categorical_value_count: How many rows have "{target_category}" in the "{query_column}" column?
Count the rows whose "{query_column}" entry is exactly "{target_category}".
How many entries in the "{query_column}" column are "{target_category}"?
Determine how many rows show "{query_column}" equal to "{target_category}".
Find the number of rows with "{target_category}" listed in the "{query_column}" column.
above_threshold_count: How many rows have a value greater than {threshold_value} in the "{query_column}" column?
Count the rows whose "{query_column}" value is greater than {threshold_value}.
How many entries in the "{query_column}" column are greater than {threshold_value}?
Determine how many rows show "{query_column}" greater than {threshold_value}.
Find the number of rows with "{query_column}" values greater than {threshold_value}.
below_threshold_count: How many rows have a value less than {threshold_value} in the "{query_column}" column?
Count the rows whose "{query_column}" value is less than {threshold_value}.
How many entries in the "{query_column}" column are less than {threshold_value}?
Determine how many rows show "{query_column}" less than {threshold_value}.
Find the number of rows with "{query_column}" values less than {threshold_value}.
interval_value_count: How many rows have a value in the inclusive interval [{interval_min}, {interval_max}] in the "{query_column}" column?
Count the rows whose "{query_column}" value falls within [{interval_min}, {interval_max}], inclusive.
How many entries in the "{query_column}" column lie between {interval_min} and {interval_max}, inclusive?
Determine how many rows show "{query_column}" within the inclusive range [{interval_min}, {interval_max}].
Find the number of rows with "{query_column}" values in [{interval_min}, {interval_max}], inclusive.
